events.headline: Super Micro, Dell and HPE have been red-hot stocks this week
events.summary: AI server makers rally on continued data center demand. SMCI up 4.2% on the day.

events.headline: Jensen Huang shakes hands with SMCI CEO Charles Liang at GTC booth
events.summary: Photo posted by Super Micro on X shows co-founder Wally Liaw standing nearby during the meeting.

events.headline: Three charged with conspiring to unlawfully divert cutting-edge U.S. AI technology to China
events.summary: DOJ unseals indictment: co-founder Wally Liaw, sales manager Steven Chang, and contractor Willy Sun charged with smuggling $2.5B of AI servers to China via dummy servers and false documents.

events.headline: US charges 3 tied to Super Micro Computer with helping smuggle billions of dollars of AI chips to China
events.summary: Liaw arrested in Northern District of California. Chang remains a fugitive in Taiwan. SMCI stock falls 8% after hours.

events.headline: Super Micro Computer issues statement on action by U.S. Attorney's Office
events.summary: Company not named as defendant. Liaw and Chang placed on administrative leave. Contractor Sun terminated. Company cooperating with investigation.

events.headline: Super Micro co-founder charged with smuggling, departs board
events.summary: Wally Liaw removed from board of directors. Scheme involved $510M of servers diverted to China in a single month (Apr-May 2025).

events.headline: Supermicro stock plunges 33% after US charges co-founder with conspiracy to smuggle Nvidia chips to China
events.summary: SMCI shares crater from $42.50 to $28.48 in two days of trading. Largest single-week decline since 2024 accounting scandal.

events.headline: Nvidia emphasizes strict compliance with export laws
events.summary: "Unlawful diversion of controlled U.S. computers to China is a losing proposition. Nvidia does not provide any service or support for such systems."

events.headline: Super Micro sued by shareholders over China-related criminal case against co-founder
events.summary: Securities fraud class action filed. Shareholders allege company failed to disclose material risks related to export control violations.

events.headline: Why Super Micro stock popped on Wednesday
events.summary: Brief relief rally of 6% on bargain hunting before lawsuit news emerged. Gains reversed by close.

events.headline: Chip Security Bill advances in House after Super Micro case
events.summary: Bipartisan legislation to strengthen AI chip export controls fast-tracked following SMCI indictment. New compliance requirements for server manufacturers.

events.headline: Super Micro faces a securities fraud lawsuit. The stock is falling.
events.summary: SMCI down another 5.3% as class action lawsuit details emerge. Analysts downgrade price targets.

events.headline: AI stock suffers more losses as legal woes resume
events.summary: Options market pricing in continued downside. Put/call ratio at 2.1, highest since November 2024.

events.headline: Chinese universities with military links bought Super Micro servers with restricted AI chips
events.summary: Investigation reveals scope of diversion extended to Chinese military-affiliated institutions. DOJ considering additional charges.
